Why they didn't contact the police is unclear, and his whereabouts after he left were unknown. As for those who met their tragic end inside the garage, a letter written by a forensic veterinarian later explained that due to the state of decomposition, necropsy wouldn't have been able to reveal a definitive cause of death for the animals, attributing their causes of death to a combination of starvation, lack of nutrition, and or dehydration, and that the dog decomposing by the overhead door was likely cannibalized by the living dogs in an attempt by them to stay alive. In her summary, she writes, the animals, both living and deceased, suffered immeasurably and describes their experience as akin to torture. Ultimately, the deceased dogs were taken by the humane society for disposal. A warrant to arrest Andre was requested for suspicion of five counts of animal cruelty in the first degree and four counts of animal cruelty in the second degree. According to a Facebook post from the city of Battleground, Washington, on March 7th, 2025, 35-year-old Andre Twilliger was located and taken into custody hours after their initial post for information from the public. It's unclear what charges he faces, if any, at this time. On December 13th, 2024, all four of the living dogs reportedly gained weight and were determined to have no medical concerns. All four were later adopted.
